summary:
The primary agents used include phenolic solutions or a combination with other effective biocides due to their ability to eliminate multiple pathogens effectively when applied correctly according to manufacturer instructions; these are commonly recommended practices across healthcare settings including hospitals' infection control protocols.

The most suitable option among those listed appears as 'A', which likely refers specifically to phenols given its known efficacy against diverse microorganisms present after an incident involving fresh human blood contamination requiring immediate attention from trained staff utilizing personal protective equipment while adhering strictly to safety guidelines provided by product manufacturers during application processes designed not only for cleanliness but also patient & worker health protection measures necessary within such environments where biohazards may arise unexpectedly necessitating prompt action without delay once identified through proper training procedures established beforehand at each facility involved directly caring patients who might experience bleeding episodes leading them onto operating theaters thus increasing risk factors associated therewith if left unaddressed promptly enough thereby ensuring overall sanitation standards remain high throughout entire institution regardless location size capacity type specialty services offered therein amongst many variables considered important here today discussing this topic further now moving forward next step would be considering additional steps beyond just initial response phase outlined above focusing more closely upon long term maintenance strategies needed sustainably over time maintaining optimal levels hygiene quality assurance metrics regularly monitored reviewed adjusted accordingly whenever deemed advisable based current best available scientific knowledge regarding infectious
